steps.content: Force is defined as the product of mass and acceleration: F = mass × acceleration.
Substitute the given values: mass = 36 kg, acceleration = a m/s$^2$. So F = 36 × a = 36a.

Evaluate all choices:
  • $F = 36a$: F = 36a. This is mass (36) times acceleration (a), exactly matching F = ma. That matches the correct answer - A is correct.
  • $F = 36 + a$: F = 36 + a. Addition instead of multiplication; does not represent the product of mass and acceleration. Eliminated.
  • $F = 36 - a$: F = 36 − a. Subtraction has no basis in Newton's second law. Eliminated.
  • $F = a - 36$: F = a − 36. Reversed subtraction; dimensionally inconsistent and physically meaningless. Eliminated.
